applicable_act: BNS Section 115 / IPC Section 323 (Voluntarily Causing Hurt)
rights_summary: You have the right to get immediate medical aid and file an FIR under Section 154 of CrPC. Police must investigate causing hurt.
Threats to cause harm or death are cognizable offenses. You can demand police protection and file a formal criminal complaint.
Protects spouses and family members from physical abuse. You have the right to protection orders and free legal aid.
Theft is a cognizable offence. Police are required to file an FIR and dispatch local officers to investigate property loss.
If someone cheats you financially or breaches trust, you have the right to file a criminal complaint for fraud and cheating.

applicable_act: Information Technology Act 2000 (Section 66C Identity Theft & Cyber Fraud)
rights_summary: Cybercrime departments must register cases of online hacking, financial phishing, or identity theft. File details at cybercrime.gov.in.
Forcing someone to deliver property or money by putting them in fear of injury is a cognizable offense. Immediate police action is required.

applicable_act: BNS Section 356 / IPC Section 499 (Defamation & Character Assassination)
rights_summary: Protects citizens against false statements that harm their reputation. You can file a criminal or civil defamation case.

applicable_act: Transfer of Property Act (Section 106 Notice to Quit)
rights_summary: Landlords must issue a written 15-day notice before terminating a monthly tenancy. Arbitrary lockouts are illegal.
Protects tenants from unreasonable rent hikes and ensures they cannot be evicted without a valid decree from Rent Controller.

applicable_act: Indian Contract Act (Section 73 Breach of Contract)
rights_summary: Provides compensation for any loss or damage caused by breach of contract. Entitles citizens to sue for damages in Civil Court.
Entering another's property without permission to intimidate or commit offense is actionable under civil trespass and criminal codes.
Protects against defective goods, deficient services, and unfair trade practices. You can file a case in the Consumer Forum.

applicable_act: Right to Information Act 2005 (Section 6 Filing RTI Request)
rights_summary: Allows citizens to request information from any public authority. Government departments must respond within 30 days.
In case of a traffic accident, victims are legally entitled to claim compensation from the motor accident claims tribunal.
Children are legally obligated to maintain their elderly parents. Parents can claim monthly maintenance allowances via tribunals.
Employees in service for 1+ year must receive 1-month notice in writing indicating reasons or payment in lieu before retrenchment.

applicable_act: Payment of Wages Act (Section 5 Time of Payment)
rights_summary: Wages must be paid before the 7th of each month (establishments under 1000 employees). Unilateral salary cuts or delays are illegal.
Mandates an Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) in all offices with 10+ employees. Victims have rights to paid leave during inquiry.

applicable_act: Maternity Benefit Act 2013 (Section 5 Rights to Leave)
rights_summary: Women employees are entitled to 26 weeks of paid maternity leave. Terminating an employee during maternity leave is a criminal offense.
No adult worker shall be required to work in a factory for more than 48 hours in any week, and is entitled to overtime pay.
Employer must deposit EPF contributions regularly. Delay or default is a punishable offense under labor laws.
